steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: In a liquid, the particles are…
options: Fixed in position and vibrating about fixed points
Very far apart with almost no forces between them
Close together but able to move past each other
Arranged in a perfect regular lattice
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! In a liquid, particles are close together (similar spacing to a solid) but they can flow and move past each other — that is why liquids take the shape of their container. Solids have particles fixed in a lattice; gases have particles widely spaced and moving freely.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Liquid particles are close together but free to move past one another, which is why liquids flow and take the shape of their container.
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.3.1 — Changes of state

steps.title: States of Matter
steps.bodyHTML: Matter exists in three states: solid (particles in a regular lattice, vibrating in fixed positions), liquid (particles close together but able to flow past each other), and gas (particles far apart, moving randomly and rapidly in all directions). The state of a substance depends on the balance between the thermal energy of the particles and the strength of the forces between them.

relationships: Solid → fixed positions, regular lattice, vibrate only
Liquid → close together, can flow past each other
Gas → far apart, random rapid motion

commonErrors: Thinking liquid particles are far apart (they are nearly as close as solid particles)
Saying gas particles are stationary between collisions
Confusing the state of a substance with its temperature only (pressure also matters)

aqa.questionText: Describe the arrangement and movement of particles in a solid.

steps.label: Describe the arrangement
steps.content: Particles are arranged in a regular pattern (lattice) and are close together with strong forces between them.

steps.label: Describe the movement
steps.content: The particles cannot move from their fixed positions but vibrate around those fixed points.
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — vibrate about fixed positions / cannot flow

aqa.finalAnswer: Particles in a solid are in a regular lattice, close together, and vibrate about fixed positions.

aqa.questionText: Which statement correctly describes the particles in a gas?

options.label: A
options.text: Close together, moving past each other

options.label: C
options.text: Far apart, moving rapidly in random directions

options.label: D
options.text: Close together, vibrating in fixed positions

markSchemeSteps: Gas particles are far apart and move randomly at high speed ✓
aqa.commonMistake: Option A describes a liquid, and option D describes a solid.
